title: Event-Specific Pit-Utility Residual Using Inferred Degradation Slope
summary: Strategy references frame stop timing as a tradeoff between current degradation, pit loss, and whether fresh tyres can repay that loss via undercut/finish utility.
rationale: Strategy references frame stop timing as a tradeoff between current degradation, pit loss, and whether fresh tyres can repay that loss via undercut/finish utility. That suggests a more dynamic event-specific utility score than current static compound windows.
implementation_hint: Infer a race-level pit-loss proxy from observed `PitStop=1` lap-time disruptions, then build features like `estimated_deg_slope x expected_remaining_laps`, `utility_of_staying_out`, `utility_of_pitting_now`, and `distance_to_optimal_stint` using slope estimates from prior laps in the current stint. Cross-fit all race-level priors inside training folds only.
expected_effect: Could separate ‘old tyres but still efficient to stay out’ from ‘same tyre age but economically optimal to stop now’, giving a cleaner next-lap stop boundary.
risk: Pit-loss inference may be noisy because raw lap time includes traffic/incidents; bad race-level priors could hurt more than help.
